macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: $171 Million in Crypto Liquidations Over Past 24 Hours
items.summary: Cryptocurrency markets saw $171 million in liquidations, with $101 million from long positions and $70.4 million from shorts; Bitcoin liquidations hit $2.07 million and Ethereum $1.71 million, affecting 82,120 traders. The largest single liquidation was $3.58 million on Hyperliquid’s BTC-USD pair.

items.title: Bitcoin Trades Near $80K Amid Institutional Demand Surge
items.summary: Bitcoin fluctuated between $76,960 and $78,663 in the past 24 hours, currently at $78,105, up 0.86% as of 11:30 AM UTC. Institutional demand continues to drive upward momentum.

items.title: Crypto Market Cap Dips 0.6% to $2.59 Trillion
items.summary: Total cryptocurrency market capitalization fell 0.6% over the past 24 hours to $2.59 trillion. Zcash led gains with a 9.1% increase amid broader market awaiting stock market signals.

items.title: 3 Million PI Tokens Moved to Exchanges, Signaling Potential Crash
items.summary: Pi Network saw nearly 3 million PI tokens transferred to centralized exchanges in 24 hours, raising exchange balances to 508 million and hinting at bearish sell-off pressure. Upcoming unlocks of 200 million coins, peaking at 20.9 million on May 1, add to downside risks despite recent ecosystem updates.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Releases Images 2.0 Image Generation Model
items.summary: OpenAI launched Images 2.0, a new image generation model hailed as impressive but with some identified problems upon testing. This update is part of recent AI advancements covered in weekly updates.[1]

items.title: Google Introduces Workspace Intelligence for Gemini
items.summary: Google released Workspace Intelligence, integrating knowledge from Gmail, Drive, and Keep into Gemini chats, demonstrated in Sheets, Canvas, Docs, and more. It enhances productivity across Google Workspace tools.[1]

items.title: Anthropic Launches Live Artifacts for Claude
items.summary: Anthropic introduced Live Artifacts for Claude, a feature that could significantly improve user workflows and efficiency. It's highlighted as an under-discussed but important release.[1]

items.title: China's DeepSeek Unveils Flagship AI Model Challenging OpenAI and Anthropic
items.summary: DeepSeek rolled out preview versions of a new flagship AI model, claiming it as the most powerful open-source platform to rival leaders like OpenAI and Anthropic. This follows their previous disruptions in AI.[2]

items.title: Meta to Lay Off 10% of Workforce to Prioritize AI Investments
items.summary: Meta announced layoffs of about 8,000 employees and cancellation of 6,000 open roles to focus resources on AI development and efficiency. The company is also introducing parental tools for teen AI usage.[4]

items.title: US Accuses China of Industrial-Scale AI Secrets Theft
items.summary: The US claims China is running large-scale campaigns to steal American AI technology, heightening tensions ahead of President Trump's Beijing visit. Experts call it part of a critical AI arms race.[3]

items.title: Ford Integrates AI Vision System in Truck Plant Operations
items.summary: Ford's Dearborn Truck Plant deployed an AI Vision system to enhance factory operations without replacing workers. Plant manager emphasizes its role in improving efficiency across applications.[5]

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Remake 'Resynced' Officially Announced for July 9 Launch on PC
items.summary: Ubisoft has confirmed the remake of Assassin's Creed Black Flag, titled Resynced, launching on July 9 including PC with system requirements analyzed.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass Price Drops as Company Reevaluates Exclusivity Strategy
items.summary: Xbox lowered Game Pass prices following critique from former PS boss, and is now openly discussing reevaluation of game exclusivity amid major industry shifts.

items.title: Stop Killing Games Campaign Secures First EU Parliament Hearing
items.summary: The Stop Killing Games initiative achieved a milestone with their first hearing in the EU Parliament, pushing for regulations against game delistings.

items.title: PSN Rolls Out Age Verification in UK and Ireland Amid PS Store IP Issues
items.summary: PlayStation Network is implementing age verification in the UK and Ireland, requiring additional user data, while facing ongoing PS Store problems with infringing games.

items.title: The Division 2 Weekend Update: Free Ronin Gloves, New Farm, and Triple XP
items.summary: The Division 2 offers free Ronin Gloves unlock, a new Ronin farm method, and triple XP event for the weekend.

items.title: PS6 Ray Tracing Performance Rumors and PS5 US Sales Uptick in March
items.summary: Rumors detail expected PS6 ray tracing capabilities, while PS5 US sales saw partial increases in March; other notes include Marathon as 6th best-selling game YTD.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Meta to Lay Off 10% of Workforce Amid AI Investments
items.summary: Meta will lay off 8,000 employees, or 10% of its workforce, next month as it invests billions in AI technology. The company announced the cuts in an internal memo, prioritizing AI advancements under CEO Mark Zuckerberg.

items.title: Meta and Amazon Strike Multibillion-Dollar Chip Rental Deal
items.summary: Meta Platforms and Amazon have agreed to a multibillion-dollar, multiyear deal for Meta to rent hundreds of thousands of Amazon's Graviton chips for AI inference needs. The partnership was reported 5 hours ago.

items.title: Iran Strike Damages Amazon Cloud Facility in Bahrain
items.summary: An Iranian strike hit an AWS-linked cloud facility in Bahrain following threats to US tech infrastructure, signaling a shift toward targeting cloud services. The incident highlights escalating geopolitical risks to tech operations.
